methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $120M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Gecko Robotics is a Series C-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $120M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising eight private firms and one public comparable (277810.KQ). Key private peers include Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity, reflecting the competitive set across Series B through Series D funding stages.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $120M total raised proxy, yielding $204M. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company cohort establishes a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The peer set includes Hadrian at $1.6B (Series C), Exotec at $2.0B (Series D), and 1X Technologies at $10.0B (Series B), illustrating sector volatility tied to technology differentiation and market timing.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $173M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $902M. The substantial gap between stage-progression ($204M) and peer-median ($1.6B) results reflects uncertainty around whether Gecko's market position and growth trajectory align with the upper or lower quartile of the peer set. Key variables include unit economics relative to peers, customer concentration, and the addressable market within the automation and inspection subsector.
